item.title: 欧盟可通过"欧洲制造"计划大幅降低本土电池价格，T&E报告称
item.description: 运输与环境组织（T&E）周一在一份报告中指出，扩大欧洲电池产能可将欧盟制造电池与来自中国的电池之间的成本差距从目前的90%缩小至约30%。该组织敦促欧盟通过"欧洲制造"计划支持该行业。【原文：Scaling up production in Europe could cut the cost gap between EU-made batteries and those coming from China to around 30% from a current 90%, transport and environment campaign group T&E said in a report on Monday, and it urged the EU to support the sector with its "Made in Europe" plans.】

item.title: 2月丹麦特斯拉注册量同比下降18%
item.description: 丹麦交通部周一公布的数据显示，2月份丹麦新注册特斯拉汽车数量同比下降18%，至419辆。【原文：Registrations of new Tesla cars in Denmark fell 18% in February from a year earlier, to 419 vehicles, data from Mobility Denmark showed on Monday.】

item.title: 晨间竞标：全球石油贸易陷入困境
item.description: 韦恩·科尔为您解析欧洲及全球市场今日展望。【原文：A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Wayne Cole.】

item.title: 丰田计划提高对集团公司TICO的收购报价，并再次延长收购期限。
item.description: 丰田汽车周一提交的文件显示，该公司计划在特定条件下将对集团旗下企业丰田工业（TICO）的收购要约价格提高至每股20,600日元（约合132美元），并将收购截止日期再次延长至3月16日。【原文：Toyota plans to raise its tender offer price for group firm Toyota Industries , or TICO, to 20,600 yen ($132) per share under certain conditions and extend the deadline again for the offer to March 16, a filing showed on Monday.】

item.title: 澳大利亚证券交易所物色新掌门人，诉讼与监管漏洞阴云笼罩
item.description: 在澳大利亚证券交易所面临困境之际，新一任掌门人的上任之路注定充满波折。与交易结算系统改造相关的诉讼以及诸多监管问题，正笼罩着该交易所的前景。【原文：As a change of guard looms over the embattled Australian stock exchange , the new chief's debut will be anything but smooth with a lawsuit linked to revamping a trade settlement system and a number of regulatory issues clouding its outlook.】

item.title: 韩国总统李明博将与新加坡总理黄循财举行峰会，商讨人工智能与科技合作事宜。
item.description: 韩国总统李在明将于周一在新加坡进行国事访问期间，与新加坡总理黄循财举行峰会，旨在拓展人工智能和核能等领域的合作。【原文：South Korean President Lee Jae Myung will meet Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong on Monday for a summit aimed at expanding cooperation in fields such as artificial intelligence and nuclear energy, during a state visit to the city-state.】

item.title: 华尔街本周展望：人工智能颠覆阴云笼罩市场，美国就业数据即将公布
item.description: 人工智能颠覆各行业的前景，将在未来一周持续令美国股市保持紧张状态。华尔街正密切关注这项新兴技术将如何在经济领域引发连锁反应。【原文：Prospects for artificial intelligence to disrupt business sectors should keep the U.S. stock market on edge in the coming week, as Wall Street looks for more insight into how the emerging technology will reverberate through the economy.】

item.title: 美元走强，欧元疲软，伊朗战争推高能源价格
item.description: 周一，欧元下跌，瑞士法郎上涨，美元跳涨。此前美国和以色列对伊朗发动空袭，炸死了最高领袖阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊，由此引发权力真空，并加剧了中东地区爆发长期战争的风险，投资者纷纷寻求避险资产。【原文：The euro slid, Swiss franc rose and the dollar jumped on Monday as investors headed for safety after the U.S. and Israel bombed Iran, killing supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei to open a power vacuum and raise the risk of a protracted Mideast war.】

item.title: 亚洲油价飙升，股市承压
item.description: 周一油价飙升，投资者纷纷涌向债券和黄金避险，因中东军事冲突似乎将持续数周之久，而此前市场已因人工智能和银行业担忧而受到惊吓。【原文：Oil prices surged on Monday and investors stampeded to the safety of bonds and gold as military conflict in the Middle East looked set to last weeks, just as markets had been spooked by AI and banking fears.】

item.title: 澳大利亚航空公司股价因美伊冲突暴跌逾10%
item.description: 澳大利亚国家航空公司澳洲航空的股价周一暴跌逾10%，跌至十个月低点。此前美国和以色列周末对伊朗发动大规模空袭，导致油价飙升。【原文：Shares of Australia's flag carrier Qantas Airways slumped more than 10% to their lowest level in 10 months on Monday after the U.S. and Israel launched large-scale strikes on Iran over the weekend, sending oil prices surging.】

item.title: 油价飙升，因美伊冲突升级扰乱航运
item.description: 随着伊朗和以色列加剧袭击，油价飙升逾8%，创下数月来新高。袭击事件导致油轮受损，并扰乱了该关键产油区的石油运输。【原文：Oil prices surged more than 8% to their highest in months as Iran and Israel stepped up attacks, damaging tankers and disrupting shipments from the key producing region.】

item.title: 英国向家长征询意见：是否应禁止16岁以下青少年使用社交媒体？
item.description: 英国正就禁止16岁以下青少年使用社交媒体、限制游戏平台及人工智能聊天机器人等议题征询家长和儿童的意见。【原文：Britain is seeking the views of parents and children on whether to ban access to social media for under-16s, as well as possible restrictions on gaming platforms and artificial intelligence chatbots.】

item.title: 分析师警告：伊朗遭袭后美国汽油价格将上涨
item.description: 分析师称，由于美国与主要产油国伊朗之间的冲突导致全球石油供应中断，美国平均零售汽油价格将于周一突破每加仑3美元大关，这是三个多月来首次。【原文：U.S. average retail gasoline prices are set to break above $3 a gallon on Monday for the first time in more than three months as the conflict between the United States and major oil producer Iran interrupts global oil flows, analysts said.】

item.title: 独家：澳大利亚称或将对应用商店、搜索引擎展开人工智能时代整治行动
item.description: 澳大利亚网络监管机构表示，可能要求搜索引擎和应用商店屏蔽未能验证用户年龄的人工智能服务。此前路透社调查发现，超过半数服务商未在截止日期前公布任何合规措施。【原文：Australia's internet regulator said it may push search engines and app stores to block artificial intelligence services that fail to verify user ages after a Reuters review found more than half had not made public any steps to comply by a deadline next week.】

item.title: 亚马逊印度大幅削减卖家推荐费以推动零售业务增长
item.description: 亚马逊周一宣布，将停止向印度卖家收取1000卢比（约合10.98美元）以下商品的推荐费。此举旨在吸引更多商家入驻其在线市场，并在印度竞争激烈的电商行业中巩固地位。【原文：Amazon will stop charging sellers in India referral fees for products under 1,000 rupees ($10.98), the company said on Monday, as it looks to attract more merchants to its online marketplace and get a stronger footing in the country's competitive e-commerce industry.】

item.title: 亚马逊云服务部门报告火灾，因物体撞击阿联酋数据中心
item.description: 亚马逊云服务部门AWS周日表示，其位于阿联酋的数据中心因遭物体撞击引发火花和火灾，导致该设施电力供应暂时中断。【原文：Amazon's cloud unit, AWS, said on Sunday that power to its data center in the United Arab Emirates was shut down temporarily after objects struck the facility, triggering sparks and a fire.】

item.title: 避险资产日元和瑞士法郎在周末伊朗袭击事件后走强
item.description: 避险货币瑞士法郎和日元走强，欧元则下跌。此前美国和以色列在周末对伊朗发动了数十年来最猛烈的袭击，击毙了最高领袖阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊，市场交易随之重启。【原文：The safe-haven Swiss franc and Japanese yen firmed, while the euro slid as trading resumed after a weekend that saw the United States and Israel launch their most ambitious attacks on Iran in decades, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.】

item.title: 黑客攻击伊朗应用程序和网站，此前美以发动空袭
item.description: 据网络安全专家和观察人士称，周六凌晨，在美以两国联合袭击伊朗境内目标的同时，一场由网络技术驱动的行动浪潮也同步展开。【原文：A wave of cyber-enabled operations took place early Saturday morning alongside the joint U.S.-Israeli attack on targets across Iran, according to cybersecurity experts and observers.】

item.title: 投资者为中东战争引发的更大反弹做准备
item.description: 中东冲突已从边缘风险演变为投资者最忧心的问题。伊朗政权更迭的可能引发的权力斗争以及地区战争的长期化前景令投资者不安，其影响将波及全球贸易乃至通胀等方方面面。【原文：From being just a fringe risk, conflict in the Middle East has become a top worry for investors unsettled by the prospect of a power struggle in Iran and a protracted regional war, with ramifications for everything from global trade to inflation.】

item.title: 海湾企业受重创，伊朗袭击引发区域性停摆
item.description: 伊朗在波斯湾的报复性打击引发了该地区自疫情以来最广泛的商业中断，迫使机场关闭、港口停运，并在金融市场引发冲击波。【原文：Iran's retaliatory strikes across the Gulf have triggered the most widespread business disruption in the region since the pandemic, forcing airport closures, halting port operations and sending shockwaves through financial markets.】

item.title: 投资者寻求黄金避风港，因美国和以色列对伊朗发动袭击
item.description: 美国和以色列对伊朗的打击加剧了全球市场的动荡，投资者正密切关注可能涌入黄金市场的避险资金。以下是投资者、交易员和分析师的反应：【原文：The U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran have intensified uncertainty across global markets, with investors closely watching potential safe-haven flows into bullion. Here is some reaction from investors, traders and analysts:】

item.title: 分析师称，受伊朗冲突影响，油价飙升10%，可能飙升至每桶100美元。
item.description: 在德黑兰警告船只勿通过霍尔木兹海峡后，多数油轮船东、大型石油公司及贸易公司已暂停经该水道运输原油、燃料及液化天然气的业务。【原文：Most tanker owners, big oil companies and trading houses suspended crude oil, fuel and liquefied natural gas shipments via the Strait of Hormuz after Tehran warned ships against moving through the waterway.】

item.title: 比亚迪2月汽车销量跌幅创疫情以来最大
item.description: 中国电动汽车制造商比亚迪上月全球销量创下六年最大跌幅。【原文：Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD recorded the biggest fall in global sales in six years last month.】

item.title: 欧佩克+讨论增产石油，因美国对伊朗的战争扰乱了石油运输
item.description: 两名欧佩克+消息人士称，该组织将于周日考虑实施超出预期的增产计划。此前，美国与以色列对欧佩克+成员国伊朗发动军事行动，德黑兰随即采取报复措施，导致中东地区石油运输中断。【原文：OPEC+ will consider a larger-than-expected oil output increase on Sunday, two OPEC+ sources said after the U.S.-Israeli war on OPEC+ member Iran and Tehran's retaliation led to shipment disruptions in the Middle East.】

item.title: 目击者称，迪拜和多哈连续第二天听到爆炸声浪
item.description: 目击者称，周日迪拜和卡塔尔首都多哈连续第二天传出巨大爆炸声。随着伊朗对美国和以色列袭击伊斯兰共和国的报复性打击范围扩大，伊朗对邻近海湾国家的反击行动也在持续。【原文：Loud blasts were heard in Dubai and the Qatari capital Doha for the second day on Sunday, witnesses said, as Iran's retaliatory strikes on neighbouring Gulf states in response to U.S. and Israeli strikes on the Islamic Republic widened.】

item.title: 小米表示，2月份电动汽车交付量突破2万辆，较1月份有所下降。
item.description: 中国电动汽车制造商小米周日通过其官方微博账号发布消息称，2月份交付量超过2万辆，较1月份的3.9万余辆有所下降。【原文：Chinese electric vehicle maker Xiaomi said it delivered more than 20,000 vehicles in February, down from more than 39,000 in January, according to a post on its official Weibo account on Sunday.】

item.title: 韩国2月出口超预期，连续第九个月增长
item.description: 韩国出口在2月份延续了连续九个月的增长势头，强劲的芯片销售持续支撑着整体出货量，尽管美国关税的不确定性给前景蒙上阴影。【原文：South Korea's exports extended their growth streak to a ninth month in February as strong chip sales continued to underpin overall shipments even as uncertainties regarding U.S. tariffs cloud the outlook.】

item.title: OpenAI详述与美国国防部协议中的分层保护机制
item.description: OpenAI周六表示，其前日与五角大楼达成的协议包含额外保障措施，以保护该技术在美国国防部机密网络中的应用场景。【原文：OpenAI said on Saturday that the agreement it struck a day ago with the Pentagon to deploy technology on the U.S. defense department's classified network includes additional safeguards to protect its use cases.】

item.title: 伯克希尔首席执行官阿贝尔在接替巴菲特后，力图安抚股东
item.description: 伯克希尔·哈撒韦公司新任首席执行官格雷格·阿贝尔于周六发表其首封致股东年度信函，试图为这家综合企业集团打上个人烙印。他在信中承诺将维持公司"堡垒般的"资产负债表，并恪守其前任兼导师沃伦·巴菲特的价值观。【原文：Berkshire Hathaway's new Chief Executive Greg Abel moved to put his stamp on the conglomerate with his first annual letter to shareholders on Saturday, pledging to maintain its "fortress-like" balance sheet and uphold the values of his predecessor and mentor, Warren Buffett.】

item.title: 据消息人士称，随着美国对伊朗发动袭击，石油和天然气巨头及贸易商已暂停经霍尔木兹海峡的运输。
item.description: 多家油轮船东、石油巨头和贸易公司已暂停经霍尔木兹海峡运输原油、燃料及液化天然气的业务。此前美国和以色列对伊朗发动袭击，德黑兰方面宣布关闭该海峡航道。贸易消息人士周六透露了这一情况。【原文：Several tanker owners, oil majors and trading houses have suspended crude oil, fuel and liquefied natural gas shipments via the Strait of Hormuz after the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran and Tehran said it had closed navigation, trading sources said on Saturday.】
